channel.title: Research Work on Utsab's Digital Garden
channel.description: Recent content in Research Work on Utsab's Digital Garden

item.title: Information Theory on Repr Learning
item.description: NeurIPS Information Theory for Representation Learning To Compress or Not to Compress- Self-Supervised Learning and Information Theory: A Review builds upon the info bottleneck principle They use a two-channel setup with the inputs to study a multiviewed system. They are essentially trying to understand what makes models learn implicitely through self-supervision without supervised labels. So they have two different channels for the input X to flow through and get encoded to Z.

item.title: Novel Depth Esitmation(Edge focussed)
item.description: Tiny Marigold - Depth - Paper Take inspiration from the marigold paper use a Vae - stable diffusion or some other(compare different if possible) lightweight Denoising End result light weight diffusion based depth estimation. Resources https://arxiv.org/pdf/2305.15798 https://github.com/Nota-NetsPresso/BK-SDM https://github.com/toshas/mdec_benchmark https://github.com/prs-eth/Marigold https://arxiv.org/pdf/2505.09358 https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10462-024-10800-8 [Marigold: Repurposing Diffusion-Based Image Generators for Monocular Depth Estimation](https://marigoldmonodepth.github.io/ AnyDepth: Depth Estimation Made Easy https://zju3dv.github.io/InfiniDepth/ [2510.07316] Pixel-Perfect Depth with Semantics-Prompted Diffusion Transformers Possible Directions zero-shot nature prior influence stable-diffusion pre-image light-weight for sure super-resolution use inns in the upsampling part?
